llm_keywords.evidence: |I| ranges from 4.666e-16 A at -0.9 V down to 6.899e-103 A at +0.1 V and up to 2.399e-5 A at -6 V, covering about 97.43 decades overall.
On both sides of zero, |I| generally increases as |V| increases: for example from 8.897e-103 A at -0.1 V to 2.399e-5 A at -6 V, and from 6.899e-103 A at +0.1 V to 2.249e-5 A at +6 V.

Matched |V| values show similar |I| magnitudes, such as 6 V: 2.399e-5 A vs 2.249e-5 A, 1 V: 6.619e-15 A vs 7.830e-15 A, and 0.2 V: 1.744e-54 A vs 1.731e-54 A for negative and positive bias respectively.

At larger |V| the sequence is not strictly monotonic point-to-point; for example on the positive side |I| changes from 1.856e-5 A at 5.45 V to 1.795e-5 A at 5.50 V to 1.300e-5 A at 5.55 V before rising again.
ln(|I|/V^2) becomes less negative with increasing |V|, moving from about -230.63 at +0.1 V and -230.38 at -0.1 V to about -14.29 near +6 V and -14.22 near -6 V.
